name: Salsa Cheese Bake
recipeIngredient: 16 oz Monterey jack cheese (cut into 1/4-inch slices)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook and stir garlic in oil in a 1-quart saucepan until garlic is brown, about 1 minute.
2. Stir in salsa, chopped green chilies, raisins, cinnamon, cloves, and cumin. Heat to boiling, stirring occasionally, then reduce heat.
3. Simmer uncovered, stirring occasionally, until the mixture is slightly thickened, about 3 minutes.
4. Cook and stir sausage in a 10-inch skillet until brown; drain.
5. Stir in 1 cup of the salsa mixture into the skillet with the cooked sausage.
6. Place half of the cheese slices in a single layer in an ungreased 1-quart shallow casserole or pie plate (9 x 1 1/4 inches).
7. Spoon the sausage mixture over the cheese slices in the casserole.
8. Top with remaining cheese slices.
9. Bake uncovered at 400°F until cheese is melted, 8 to 10 minutes.
10. Heat remaining salsa mixture and olives until hot; pour over the cheese.
11. Serve with tortilla chips. (15 servings; 295 calories per serving.)
